items.title: Retok: Token-efficiency analyzer for Claude Code and Codex CLI (zero deps)
items.why_it_matters: A zero-dependency token analyzer built specifically for Claude Code and Codex CLI users. Since you use both tools daily, this lets you audit your terminal sessions to see exactly where your token budget is going. You can run this on your AI-ingest repository today to identify which files are bloating your context window and trim them down.

items.title: Show HN: A hook that stops Claude Code from re-trying fixes that failed
items.why_it_matters: A Git hook that prevents Claude Code from getting stuck in infinite loops trying the same failed bug fixes over and over. Since you're directing Claude to write your code, this stops it from burning through your token limit when it hits a wall. You can drop this hook into your AI-ingest repo this week to automatically abort repetitive, failing Claude commands.

items.title: Show HN: cc-context-telemetry - context and rate-limit % in Claude Code's bar
items.why_it_matters: An extension that adds real-time context window and rate-limit tracking directly to your Claude Code status bar. This takes the guesswork out of when Claude is about to forget your project's context or hit an API limit. Install it in your terminal today to keep an eye on your usage while building out the next feature of AI-ingest.

items.title: Show HN: Sokkan – my Claude Code sessions kept forgetting, so I gave them memory
items.why_it_matters: A tool designed to solve the 'forgetfulness' of Claude Code sessions by giving them persistent memory across restarts. This is highly useful when you're working on multi-file changes in Next.js and don't want to re-explain your database schema every time you start a new session. Try running your next feature build through Sokkan to see if it keeps Claude aligned on your Supabase setup.

items.title: Tile-pack repos, files, and prompts into images for 60–75% fewer Claude tokens
items.why_it_matters: A tool that packs your code repositories, documentation, and prompts into images, leveraging Claude's vision capabilities to reduce token consumption by up to 75%. If you are running into tight token limits on your Next.js project, you can use this to bundle your utility files into a single image and pass it to Claude to see if it maintains the same coding accuracy at a fraction of the cost.
